final_message:
All five functions are implemented in `problems.py` and every check passes, including the docstring examples, the listed edge cases, and a brute-force cross-check of `rolling_max` (1M elements with window 5000 runs in ~0.4s).

Implementation notes:

- **`parse_duration`** — anchored regex `(?:(\d)h)?(?:(\d)m)?(?:(\d)s)?` using `[0-9]` (so Unicode digits don't sneak in), requiring at least one component to match. Non-strings, whitespace, wrong order, duplicates, bare units, negatives, and trailing garbage all raise `ValueError`.
- **`luhn_valid`** — strips spaces/hyphens, returns `False` for empty results or any non-`0-9` character (never raises, even on non-string input), then the standard right-to-left double-every-second-digit checksum.
- **`flatten`** — recursive walk building joined key paths with `sep`; empty nested dicts contribute nothing, non-dict values (lists included) pass through untouched, and the input is never mutated.
- **`top_k_frequent`** — `Counter` plus a sort keyed on `(-count, word)` for the alphabetical tie-break; `k <= 0` returns `[]`, oversized `k` returns all distinct words.
- **`rolling_max`** — monotonic deque of indices for O(n) time; `window <= 0` raises `ValueError`, `window > len(nums)` returns `[]`.
